algorithm.name: QuranFSRS-Implicit — an FSRS-4.5-derived scheduler whose grade is inferred from MCQ outcome + latency instead of a self-rating tap
algorithm.basis: FSRS (Free Spaced Repetition Scheduler) is the right modern base because it models two independent variables per card — Stability (S, how long memory survives) and Difficulty (D, intrinsic hardness) — and schedules the next review at the moment recall probability decays to a target retrievability (e.g. R=0.90). It beats SM-2 for exactly this population: casual learners who forget, because it recovers gracefully from lapses instead of nuking the whole interval, and it lets us tune desired retention (Quran demands high R, ~0.92-0.94, higher than a language app). BUT vanilla FSRS depends on the human self-rating Again/Hard/Good/Easy. For a casual Muslim who cannot verify their own Arabic recall, self-grading is both high-friction AND unreliable (people inflate). So we keep FSRS's S/D math and replace the human grade with an IMPLICIT grade derived from the MCQ: the forced choice IS the memory test, and its correctness plus response latency deterministically produce the FSRS rating. This is the Monoxer insight (no self-grading, machine judges) fused with FSRS's superior scheduling.
algorithm.gradingScale: IMPLICIT — the learner NEVER rates themselves. Four FSRS ratings (1 Again, 2 Hard, 3 Good, 4 Easy) are computed from two signals the app measures directly: (a) correctness of the MCQ tap, (b) response latency vs the learner's own rolling median latency for that card-tier. Mapping: WRONG tap -> Again(1). CORRECT but slow (latency > 2.0x personal median, or a self-correct after a first wrong reveal in learn mode) -> Hard(2). CORRECT at normal speed (0.5x-2.0x median) -> Good(3). CORRECT and fast (< 0.5x median AND first-try) -> Easy(4). Two guardrails on the signal: a floor of ~800ms so an accidental fat-finger fast-correct is not over-rewarded as Easy, and near-miss detection — if the wrong tap was a phonetically/orthographically confusable distractor we still grade Again but flag the confusion pair for a targeted follow-up card. Latency is normalized per question-tier (a 6-distractor whole-ayah cloze is expected to be slower than a 2-distractor single word) so escalating difficulty does not falsely read as weakening memory.
algorithm.intervalLogic:
Concrete progression for ONE word-card (say the cloze on 'alqoohu' — 'cast him [into the well]' — in Act 5), desired retention R=0.92, FSRS-style. INTRODUCTION (same session, not yet scheduled): the word is first met inside its scene, then a 2-distractor cloze. First correct in-session -> card CREATED with initial Stability from FSRS init weights for a Good first grade (~S=3.0 days) and Difficulty seeded ~5/10. GRADUATING STEPS still same-day: a fast-correct pushes it out of the session; a wrong answer re-queues it after ~3 intervening cards (learning-step lapse) and it must be answered correctly to leave the session. Then the scheduled intervals, each recomputed from live S after the grade:
- Review 1 (~1 day later): Good -> S grows via FSRS stability-increase (S ~ 3d -> ~7d). Next due ~7d.
- Review 2 (~7d): Good -> S ~7d -> ~18d.
- Review 3 (~18d): Easy (fast first-try) -> larger multiplier, S ~18d -> ~55d.
- Review 4 (~55d): Good -> S ~55d -> ~130d. The word is now effectively long-term.
LAPSE example: if at Review 2 the learner taps the wrong distractor (Again), FSRS does NOT reset to zero — it applies the post-lapse stability formula (new S is a fraction of old S, damped by D), so S ~7d collapses to ~2-3d, not to 0, and Difficulty ticks up ~+1. The card re-enters a short relearning step (~10 min in-session, then ~2d). This graceful recovery is why casual forgetters stay on the wagon instead of feeling punished back to square one. As Difficulty rises, ALL future intervals for that specific word shrink automatically — that IS the auto-adjustment. Interleaving/fuzz: a small random jitter (±5-10%) is added to each due date to prevent whole acts clumping on one calendar day.
algorithm.monoxerNotes: Borrow four things from Monoxer specifically, each mapped to a concrete mechanism here: (1) AUTO-DIFFICULTY — Monoxer silently ramps a task's hardness to the learner's ceiling. We do it two ways: FSRS Difficulty per card shrinks intervals for weak words, AND the QUESTION FORMAT escalates by tier (see below) as a card's Stability crosses thresholds, so mastery is met with a harder question, not just a longer gap. (2) FORCED RECALL, NO PASSIVE FLIP — there is no 'show answer / rate yourself' Anki flip. Every review is an MCQ the learner must actively answer; production is forced. (3) NO SELF-GRADING — correctness is adjudicated by the tap, latency is measured, and FSRS rating is derived; the user's opinion of their memory never enters the math (kills grade inflation). (4) ESCALATING FORMAT as a first-class scheduled dimension. Concretely, a word-card climbs Question Tiers as it matures: Tier 1 = 2-distractor single-word cloze with the scene image shown; Tier 2 = 4-distractor cloze, image dimmed; Tier 3 = 6-distractor cloze, no image, distractors drawn from same-root/same-act confusables (near-miss engineered); Tier 4 = multi-blank / whole-ayah cloze where the learner reassembles 2-3 words of the ayah in order; Tier 5 = 'next-ayah' recall — given ayah N choose the correct opening of ayah N+1 (this is what actually welds ayahs into continuous hifz). Tier is a function of the card's Stability band, so the SAME word gets harder as it gets more familiar — the learner is always answering at the edge of their ability, exactly the Monoxer feel, but on an FSRS clock.

philosophy: Hifz fails at retention, not acquisition — people can memorize a surah in a week and lose it in a month. So this engine optimizes for the survival curve of memory, not the thrill of a first correct answer. The core bet: make the recall event itself unavoidable and machine-judged (a forced-choice MCQ is the test, not a flashcard you flip and lie to yourself about), then schedule that event at the precise moment a word is about to be forgotten (FSRS), then wrap the whole thing in the same dopamine loops that keep people on TikTok for three hours — variable reward, streaks, progress bars, near-miss tension — but aimed at Qur'anic retention so heavy screen time becomes ibadah instead of waste. Crucially, memory is not stored as a shuffled pile of 600 word-cards; it is stored the way the brain actually keeps a story — as a MOVIE of 19 scenes. Every atomic word attaches to a scene image and an act number, so recall is reconstructive ('what happens in the pit scene? he casts him — alqoohu — into ghayabat al-jubb') rather than rote. The long game is: a year from now the learner can still walk the 19 acts in order and, act by act, produce the ayahs, because each ayah was welded to a place in a narrative they will never forget. Beautiful patience, sabr jameel, is engineered into the product: no guilt, graceful recovery from lapses, and a story so compelling you want to come back and re-watch it.
cardStates: NEW — word exists in an act but not yet unlocked (the act is still locked, or the scene not yet watched)
LEARNING — introduced this session; cycling through same-day steps until first stable correct; not yet on the FSRS clock
REVIEW (Young) — scheduled, Stability under ~21 days; still fragile, tighter intervals, Question Tier 1-2
REVIEW (Mature) — Stability over ~21 days; long intervals, Question Tier 3+, format escalates
RELEARNING (Lapsed) — was Review, learner tapped wrong; damped Stability, short relearning step, Difficulty bumped; NOT reset to zero
SUSPENDED/BURIED — temporarily out of rotation (whole-ayah welded, or leech pulled for a targeted confusion-pair drill)
WELDED — top tier: the word survives inside whole-ayah and next-ayah recall (Tier 4-5); counts as long-term retained
LEECH — repeatedly lapsed; flagged, its confusable-distractor is identified, and it is routed to a special scaffolded mini-lesson instead of endless failed reviews
sessionLoop: A session is 'watching one or two scenes of the movie', roughly 5-12 minutes. It opens on the STORY MAP — the 19-act filmstrip — with today's cursor resting on the next scene; the learner sees their streak flame, the day's review count, and a glowing 'next scene' button (variable-reward anticipation before anything loads). Tap in. If a NEW act is unlocking, the scene plays first: the sceneImage and a 15-20s narration of the act's summary and emotional beat — this is the pleasurable, low-effort hook that gets them in the door (the 'just one more scene' pull). Then the forced-recall drills begin, but they are framed as 'rebuilding this scene': cards surface in NARRATIVE ORDER for a fresh act (word by word as the ayahs unfold), so drilling literally re-tells the scene. Each card is one MCQ — read the ayah with a blank, tap the missing word from the choices, sub-second feedback with a satisfying hit/near-miss animation, and a thin progress bar filling toward 'scene complete'. Interleaved into the new-act cards are the DUE reviews from earlier acts (FSRS decides which), so the session is part new-story, part re-watch. Latency and correctness silently feed the scheduler — the learner never rates anything, just plays. Near-misses (wrong-but-confusable taps) create a small tension spike and an immediate corrective re-ask. The session ends on a resolution beat: 'Scene 5 rebuilt', the filmstrip advances with a fill animation, the streak ticks, a variable bonus may drop (a memory-hook insight card, e.g. 'notice the SHIRT appears here for the first time'), and the next scene is teased. Close the app having spent ten minutes and feeling like you watched an episode, not ground flashcards.

addictionLoops: Variable ratio reward: after a completed scene a bonus MAY drop (nothing is guaranteed) — sometimes a beautiful memory-hook insight card (the shirt-thread, the four dreams, sabr jameel echo), sometimes a rare 'golden ayah' animation, sometimes just the streak tick. Unpredictable payoff is the core slot-machine pull, repurposed toward Qur'anic tafsir micro-lessons.

Near-miss tension: engineered distractors that are same-root/same-act confusables mean a wrong tap feels like 'I almost had it', the exact near-miss dynamic that keeps gamblers pulling — but here it triggers an immediate corrective re-ask that actually cements the word.

Zeigarnik / open-loop cliffhangers: each act ends on its emotional beat mid-tension (Yusuf vanishing into the well, the torn shirt, 'I am Yusuf') and the next scene is teased but gated — the unfinished story is psychologically itchy, driving return.
Progress-bar dopamine at three nested scales: the per-scene bar (seconds), the per-act mastery ring (a session), the 19-act filmstrip lighting up (weeks) — always a bar close to filling.

Reciprocity / investment escalation: the more acts you light up, the more you feel you'd 'lose' by stopping (sunk-cost turned toward persistence), so re-watching due reviews feels like protecting an investment you're proud of.

Just-one-more-scene autoplay pull: because scene narration is low-effort and pleasurable, the app can offer 'watch the next scene?' the way binge autoplay does — but the next scene requires clearing that act's recall, so the binge pull is spent on memorization.
gameMechanics.progression: Progression is literally walking THE MOVIE. Acts unlock in sequence (Act N+1 unlocks when Act N's words reach at least Young-Review stability), so the learner physically advances through the 19-act filmstrip. Within an act, a mastery RING fills as its words climb Question Tiers; when the ring completes the scene is marked 'rebuilt' and glows on the map. Above acts sit the cross-act memory-hook COLLECTIBLES — completing the three shirt-scenes unlocks the 'Shirt Thread' badge and its dedicated interleaved deck; the four dreams unlock the 'Dream Loop'; the two sabr-jameel verses unlock the 'Beautiful Patience' pair. Final progression state: WELDED — the whole surah plays back as next-ayah recall, the eleven-stars dream of Act 2 visibly connects to the prostration of Act 18, and the filmstrip is fully lit. Levels are named after story milestones (Pit, House of al-Aziz, Prison, Treasury, Throne), so leveling up = ascending Yusuf's own arc, down-down-up-up.
gameMechanics.streaks: A daily streak flame that only requires clearing the day's DUE reviews (not a fixed card count) — so the goal auto-scales to what memory actually needs, never busywork. Critically softened for the Qur'an context: a FREEZE token (earned every 7 days) auto-protects one missed day, and a compassionate 'sabr jameel' recovery — after a break the streak is not zeroed to 0 but restored to a fraction with a one-tap 'welcome back, resume the story' path that front-loads the most-decayed cards. Streaks celebrate CONSISTENCY of return, and the app explicitly de-emphasizes raw streak length once it gets long, to prevent it becoming an idol (see guardrails).
gameMechanics.ethicalGuardrails: Because this is the Qur'an, the engagement loops are bounded by hard ethical rails, not left to run: (1) SESSION SATIETY CAP — a gentle 'you've done well, the rest is saved for tomorrow' after the day's due reviews + one new scene; the app will REFUSE to let you binge-cram 5 acts in one night, because that harms retention AND turns the Qur'an into a compulsion. FSRS backs this: extra reps today don't help a card not yet due. (2) NIYYAH & ADAB framing — sessions open with intention and the mushaf is treated with respect; no trivializing the text as mere game tokens; correct Arabic orthography and tajweed markers always shown; audio recited properly. (3) NO DARK PATTERNS — no manipulative loss-framing push notifications ('your streak DIES in 1hr!'), no pay-to-win, no shaming. Notifications are dignified reminders tied to the learner's own salah times if they opt in. (4) STREAK IDOLATRY BRAKE — once streaks are long the UI shrinks the number and elevates 'ayahs retained long-term' and understanding instead, and periodically reminds that the goal is Allah's word in the heart, not a number. (5) MEANING NOT JUST SOUND — every drilled word links to its meaning and its place in the story (forced understanding, per the founder's principle), so this is never parrot-memorization. (6) RETENTION HONESTY — the app never fakes mastery for engagement; if a word is decaying it is surfaced, because misleading someone about their hifz would be a betrayal of the text.

modelPairing:
This is the spine of the whole design: the atomic per-word cloze MCQs are never free-floating cards — every card is a child of an ACT, and the 19-act movie is the top-level index over the entire card collection. Concretely:

1) STRUCTURE: cards belong to acts, acts belong to the spine. Each word-card carries actNumber, ayahNumber, wordPositionInAyah, and a link to that act's sceneImage + emotionalBeat. Retention is reconstructive: the learner recalls 'alqoohu' not as card #187 but as 'the verb in the PIT scene where the brothers cast him down' — location in the movie is the retrieval cue.

2) SCENE-GATED UNLOCK, IN ORDER. Acts unlock strictly in sequence (Act 1 Overture -> ... -> Act 18 Prostration -> Act 19 Epilogue). An act unlocks only after the prior act's words reach Young-Review stability. You cannot drill Act 8's temptation words before you've rebuilt the pit and the sale — so the learner rebuilds the story act by act, and the surah is memorized end-to-end in narrative order by construction.

3) SCENE FIRST, THEN WORDS. When an act unlocks, its scene plays (image + narration of summary + emotional beat) BEFORE any word is drilled, so each subsequently-drilled word attaches to a live mental image. Then the act's high-value plot-carrying words are drilled first (alqoohu / ghayabat al-jubb for the pit; raawadat-hu / ma'adhallah for the temptation; qadd / dubur for the torn shirt; sab' baqaraat for the king's dream) — the verbs and nouns that ARE the plot, so the word and the story-beat encode together.

4) NARRATIVE ORDER EARLY, INTERLEAVED LATER. For a freshly unlocked act, cards surface in strict ayah/word order — drilling literally re-tells the scene, giving the beginner a coherent through-line. As words mature onto the FSRS clock, they get interleaved across acts by the scheduler (desirable difficulty), so re-watching mixes scenes — but the STORY MAP is always the frame, so even interleaved review feels like hopping around a movie you know, not a shuffled pile.

5) THE CLOZE BLANK SITS IN ITS SCENE. Each MCQ shows the ayah with one word blanked, headed by the act's sceneImage as visual context. A cloze on 'qamees' always shows WHICH of the three shirt-scenes it belongs to (false-blood / torn-from-behind / healing), forcing the learner to place the word on the shirt-thread rather than answer it in a vacuum — meaning and location are tested together.

6) MEMORY HOOKS AS CROSS-ACT DECKS. The mental model's hooks become special interleaved 'linking decks' that unlock as collectibles: the SHIRT deck (the three qamees ayahs drilled together, lie->proof->healing), the DREAMS deck (Acts 2,11,12,18 — first and last are the same dream), the SABR JAMEEL pair (Act 5 vs Act 15, contrasting the verbatim 'sawwalat lakum anfusukum amran'), the KAYD danger-word deck, the SEVEN cluster, the 'innahu la yudee'u ajra al-muhsineen' echo deck. These teach recurring vocabulary through its pattern — far stickier than isolated repetition — and give the collectible/progression layer real Qur'anic substance.

7) RING/MIRROR RETRIEVAL SCAFFOLDING. Because the surah is a ring composition (down-down-up-up; dream answered by prostration; betrayal at home answered by reunion at home), the scheduler deliberately pairs beginning-act words with their end-act echoes in the same review batch (Act 2 star-dream vocab with Act 18 prostration vocab; brothers' kayd with the women's kayd), so recalling one half cues the other — using the surah's own architecture as a mnemonic lattice.

8) REVIEW = RE-WATCHING THE MOVIE. The one-line spine is the review index: a review session is presented as walking the 19 acts, each act 'opening' to reveal its due word-cloze cards. So spaced repetition never feels like a queue — it feels like re-watching your favorite film scene by scene, which keeps recall structured, ordered, and retained for the long term.
dataModel:
Core entities (relational, e.g. Postgres):

surah (id, name, total_ayahs, one_line_spine)

act (id, surah_id, act_number, name, ayah_range_start, ayah_range_end, summary, emotional_beat, scene_image_url, narration_audio_url, unlock_rule) — the 19 acts of the mental model; the top-level index.

ayah (id, surah_id, act_id, ayah_number, arabic_text, translation, transliteration, audio_url)

word (id, ayah_id, act_id, position_in_ayah, arabic, root, translation, transliteration, is_plot_key BOOL, hook_tags JSONB) — hook_tags marks membership in cross-act decks e.g. ['shirt','dream','sabr_jameel','kayd','seven']. is_plot_key surfaces the verbs/nouns that carry the plot for first-drill priority.

card (id, word_id, act_id, question_tier INT) — one drillable unit per word; tier is the current escalation level (1 single-word 2-distractor ... 5 next-ayah recall).

distractor_pool (id, word_id, distractor_arabic, confusion_type ENUM[same_root, same_act, phonetic, orthographic]) — engineered near-miss distractors; used to build MCQ options and to detect confusion pairs on wrong taps.

user (id, name, email, desired_retention DEFAULT 0.92, prefs JSONB, niyyah_prefs)

user_card_state (id, user_id, card_id, state ENUM[new,learning,review,relearning,suspended,welded,leech], stability FLOAT, difficulty FLOAT, due_at TIMESTAMP, last_reviewed_at, reps INT, lapses INT, current_tier INT, personal_median_latency_ms INT) — the FSRS per-user-per-card memory model; this is the scheduler's heart.

review (id, user_id, card_id, reviewed_at, was_correct BOOL, latency_ms INT, chosen_distractor_id NULLABLE, question_tier, derived_rating ENUM[again,hard,good,easy], pre_stability, post_stability, pre_difficulty, post_difficulty) — the immutable log of every forced-recall event; derived_rating is the implicit grade computed from was_correct + latency. This log also powers per-user FSRS parameter optimization and all analytics.

user_act_progress (id, user_id, act_id, state ENUM[locked,scene_watched,drilling,rebuilt], mastery_ring_pct, unlocked_at, rebuilt_at) — drives scene-gated unlock and the story-map UI.

collectible / user_collectible (hook deck badges: shirt_thread, dream_loop, sabr_jameel, kayd, seven, muhsineen_echo — earned state + the interleaved deck they gate).

streak (user_id, current_len, longest_len, last_active_date, freeze_tokens, freeze_auto_used_dates JSONB) — with freeze/recovery fields for the compassionate streak rules.

session (id, user_id, started_at, ended_at, cards_seen, new_act_id NULLABLE, satiety_cap_hit BOOL) — for the session-satiety guardrail and engagement analytics.

Key relationships: surah 1-* act 1-* ayah 1-* word 1-1 card; user *-* card via user_card_state; act *-* user via user_act_progress; word *-* deck via hook_tags/collectible.
antiPatterns: Anki-style self-grading (Again/Hard/Good/Easy taps): high friction and inflated for learners who can't verify their own Arabic recall — grade must be machine-derived from MCQ correctness + latency.
Passive flip cards (see word -> reveal -> 'did I know it?'): no forced production, so no real recall event and no honest signal. Every review must be an answered MCQ.
Shuffled free-floating flashcard pile with no narrative index: destroys reconstructive memory and makes review feel like grinding. Cards must hang off the 19-act movie.
Ayah-by-ayah drilling in isolation before the scene is set: words fail to attach to imagery. Always play the scene first, then drill.
Resetting Stability to zero on a single lapse (SM-2 punishment): demoralizes casual forgetters. Use FSRS damped post-lapse stability — graceful recovery, sabr jameel.

Fixed daily card quota / letting users cram whole acts ahead of schedule: harms retention (reps before a card is due don't help) and turns the Qur'an into a compulsion. Enforce the due-based satiety cap.
Dark-pattern engagement: loss-framed 'your streak dies!' notifications, pay-to-win, shame mechanics — forbidden for a Qur'an app; use dignified, salah-time-aware reminders.
Streak-as-idol: letting a giant streak number become the point. De-emphasize length over time; elevate ayahs-retained and understanding.
Sound-only parroting with no meaning: violates 'forced understanding'. Every word links to meaning and its story location.
Faking mastery to keep engagement up: never hide a decaying card; retention honesty is non-negotiable for the Qur'an.
Static difficulty (same question format forever): wastes the Monoxer escalation lever. Question tier must climb with stability so the learner always works at their edge.
Trivializing the mushaf as mere game tokens: keep correct orthography, tajweed, proper recitation, and adab framing intact.
